annotations.description: orders-api request latency p99 has exceeded 3s for 5m

incident.title: orders-api p99 latency spike
incident.description: Orders API responses degraded, timeouts observed

transcriptEvents.text: Investigating HighLatency on orders-api. Checking application logs for timeouts.

result.name: loki
result.preview:
2026-07-05T09:14:02Z ERROR orders-api: timeout waiting for connection from pool (active=100 idle=0 max=100)
2026-07-05T09:14:03Z ERROR orders-api: timeout waiting for connection from pool (active=100 idle=0 max=100)
[repeated ~40x/min since 09:13]

transcriptEvents.text: Checking the DB connection pool saturation over time.

result.name: promql
result.preview:
09:00 42
09:10 88
09:13 100
09:14 100
09:15 100
(pool pinned at its configured max of 100 since 09:13)

transcriptEvents.text: Confirming against the database's own view of active connections.

args.cmd: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state='active';

result.name: psql
result.source: psql -c "SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state='active';"
result.preview:
count
-----
   97
(1 row)
-- orders-api pool_size=100, postgres max_connections=100, unchanged config for 30 days

transcriptEvents.text: Checking whether pod-level autoscaling is keeping up.

result.name: kubectl
result.preview:
NAME         REFERENCE               TARGETS   MINPODS   MAXPODS   REPLICAS
orders-api   Deployment/orders-api   62%/70%   3         10        6
(HPA is healthy, well below its scale-up threshold — pod count is not the bottleneck)

transcriptEvents.text: Checking the traffic level driving demand for connections.

result.name: promql
result.preview:
09:00 120/s
09:10 610/s
09:13 900/s
(traffic surge from a marketing push notification sent at 09:08 — each request holds a connection for the full request lifetime, exhausting the fixed pool_size=100)
